prompts.bulk_comment_description: Set comment for {{count}} sessions (leave empty to remove)
prompts.boost_requires_comment_prompt: Boost requires a comment. Enter a comment for {{label}}:
prompts.boost_requires_comment_unavailable_here: Boost requires a comment, but comment editing is unavailable here.
prompts.boost_comment_required: Comment is required to add boost.
prompts.boost_comment_save_failed: Failed to save comment required for boost: {{error}}
prompts.multiplier_positive: Multiplier must be a positive number.

errors.status_load_failed: Failed to load AI model status:

prompts.reset_knowledge_confirm: Reset AI knowledge? This deletes model tables and feedback history.

text.auto_safe_is_disabled_set_mode_to_auto_s: Auto-safe is disabled. Set mode to auto_safe and save settings.
text.rollback_the_last_auto_safe_batch_this_w: Rollback the last auto-safe batch? This will only revert sessions that haven't been manually changed since.
text.show_sparkle_icon_on_sessions_assigned_b: Show sparkle icon on sessions assigned by AI auto-safe
text.show_project_suggestions_for_unassigned_: Show project suggestions for unassigned sessions
text.show_the_score_details_button_barchart3_: Show the score details button (BarChart3) on each session
text.train_after_a_larger_series_of_manual_co: Train after a larger series of manual corrections, after importing new data, or when the reminder indicates that the model needs refreshing.
text.the_reminder_appears_automatically_when_: The reminder appears automatically when: you have at least {{feedbackTrigger}} new corrections or over {{retrainHours}}h passed since last training and there are new corrections.
text.mode_off_disables_suggestions_suggest_sh: Mode: off disables suggestions, suggest shows suggestions without auto-changes, auto_safe allows automatic assignments only with high confidence.
text.suggest_min_confidence_minimum_confidenc: Suggest Min Confidence: minimum confidence to show suggestion in sessions.
text.auto_safe_min_confidence_required_confid: Auto-safe Min Confidence: required confidence threshold for automatic assignment.
text.auto_safe_min_evidence_how_many_signals_: Auto-safe Min Evidence: how many signals (e.g. app/token/time history) must confirm decision.
text.session_limit_how_many_unassigned_sessio: Session Limit: how many unassigned sessions auto-safe will scan in one batch.
text.feedback_weight_how_much_manual_correcti: Feedback Weight: how much manual corrections (thumbs up/down, reassignments) influence the model during training. Higher = corrections dominate more. Default: 5.
text.start_with_mode_suggest_suggest_0_60_aut: Start with mode=suggest, suggest=0.60, auto=0.85, evidence=3. When suggestions are accurate, enable auto_safe.
text.if_auto_safe_makes_wrong_assignments_rai: If auto-safe makes wrong assignments: raise Auto-safe Min Confidence to 0.9+ or Min Evidence to 4-5.
text.estimated_cost_light_training_usually_un: Estimated cost: light training, usually under 10 seconds.
text.decay_half_life_description: How quickly old data loses influence. Shorter = model adapts faster to workflow changes. Default: 90 days.
text.configure_which_ai_indicators_and_feedba: Configure which AI indicators and feedback controls are visible on session rows.
text.rollback_only_reverts_sessions_that_have: Rollback only reverts sessions that have not been manually changed since the auto-safe run.
text.you_have_corrections_since_last_training: You have {{feedbackCount}} corrections since last training (threshold: {{threshold}}).
text.over_h_passed_since_last_training_and_th: Over {{hours}}h passed since last training and there are new corrections.
text.the_model_has_correction_data_but_has_ne: The model has correction data but has never been trained.

errors.project_folder_required: Project folder is required to identify tracked files.

confirm.exclude_project: Exclude this project? It can be restored later.
confirm.reset_project_time: Reset tracked time for this project? This cannot be undone.
confirm.compact_project_data: Compact this project's data? This will remove detailed file activity history, but will keep sessions and total time. This cannot be undone.
confirm.delete_project_permanent: Permanently delete project "{{projectLabel}}"? This will remove the project record, unassign linked apps/sessions/file activities, and delete manual sessions. This cannot be undone.
confirm.delete_all_excluded: Permanently delete ALL excluded projects? This cannot be undone.
confirm.clear_candidates: Blacklist all visible folder candidates? They will no longer appear as suggestions.
confirm.clear_all_detected: Blacklist all visible detected candidates? They will no longer appear as suggestions.

empty.no_folders_configured_hint: Add a folder above to scan for projects

warnings.no_folders_defined: Add at least one project folder to enable automatic project detection. Without folders, TIMEFLOW cannot match file activity to projects.

text.compact_this_project_s_data_this_will_re: Compact this project's data? This will remove detailed file activity history, but will keep sessions and total time. This cannot be undone.
text.failed_to_save_comment_required_for_boos: Failed to save comment required for boost:
text.apply_this_comment_to_all_sessions_in_th: Apply this comment to all sessions in this group.
text.manual_sessions_cannot_be_unassigned_the: Manual sessions cannot be unassigned (they must belong to a project). Delete them instead.
text.unassign_automatic_sessions_from_this_pr: Unassign {{count}} automatic sessions from this project?
text.enter_a_comment_for_this_session_leave_e: Enter a comment for this session (leave empty to remove).
text.multiplier_must_be_0_use_1_to_reset: Multiplier must be > 0. Use 1 to reset.
text.reset_tracked_time_for_this_project_this: Reset tracked time for this project? This cannot be undone.
text.compaction_removes_detailed_file_level_h: Compaction removes detailed file-level history while preserving sessions and total time.
text.no_sessions_found_for_this_project: No sessions found for this project.
text.bulk_action_on_sessions_apps_affected: Bulk action on {{count}} sessions - Apps affected: {{apps}}
text.applies_to_all_sessions_in_this_visual_c: Applies to all {{count}} sessions in this visual chunk

settings.splitToleranceDesc1: Split only when projects have nearly identical scores.
settings.splitToleranceDesc2: Split when second project has ≥{{pct}}% of leader's score.
settings.splitToleranceDesc3: Split even with large score disparity.
settings.splitAutoDesc: Sessions meeting split conditions will be split automatically.

license.title: License
license.no_server_url: Set the server URL before activating a license.

lan_sync.title: LAN Sync
lan_sync.description: Peer-to-peer synchronization between TIMEFLOW instances on the same local network.
lan_sync.enable_description: Starts a local HTTP server and discovers other TIMEFLOW instances on your network.
lan_sync.auto_sync_description: Automatically synchronize when another TIMEFLOW instance is found on the network.
lan_sync.no_peers: No TIMEFLOW instances found on your network. Make sure both devices are connected to the same LAN/Wi-Fi.
lan_sync.error_peer_unreachable: Peer offline — start TIMEFLOW Dashboard on the other machine
lan_sync.slave_info: This device is in slave mode — synchronization is initiated by the master.
lan_sync.firewall_hint_title: No visible peers — check your firewall
lan_sync.firewall_hint_description: If the daemon did not have administrator privileges, firewall rules may not have been added. Add them manually:

help.language_hint: Language is managed in Settings > Language.

quickstart.language_hint: Language is managed in Settings > Language.
quickstart.intro: Welcome to TIMEFLOW! Let's go through these few simple steps to properly configure your time tracking system.
quickstart.outro: That's all! Now you can enjoy full automation.

file_preparation.title: File Preparation
file_preparation.description: Place both .exe files (timeflow-dashboard.exe and timeflow-demon.exe) in the same folder and run timeflow-dashboard.exe.

projects_configuration.title: Projects Configuration
projects_configuration.description: In the Projects tab, point to the parent folder of your work. Each subfolder will be treated as a separate project.

adding_applications.title: Adding Applications
adding_applications.description: In the Applications tab, add process names (e.g. name.exe) you want to track. Give them friendly names. Do not leave the list empty, because an empty list pauses tracking instead of switching TIMEFLOW to monitor-all.

starting_daemon.title: Starting the Daemon
starting_daemon.description: In the Daemon tab, start it and enable 'Autostart ON'. The system will start working in the background.

assigning_sessions.title: Assigning Sessions
assigning_sessions.description: Right-click in the Dashboard tab to assign sessions. A star in the tray icon means unassigned sessions.

ai_training.title: AI Training
ai_training.description: A few manual assignments will train your local AI (AI & Model tab), which will then start automating the work for you.

settings_page.please_use_a_valid_hh_mm_time: Please use a valid HH:mm time.
settings_page.to_time_must_be_later_than_from_time: 'To' time must be later than 'From' time.
settings_page.are_you_sure_you_want_to_delete_all_data: Are you sure you want to delete all data? This cannot be undone.
settings_page.demo_mode_enabled_dashboard_now_uses_the: Demo mode enabled. Dashboard now uses the demo database.
settings_page.demo_mode_disabled_dashboard_now_uses_th: Demo mode disabled. Dashboard now uses the primary database.
settings_page.last_manual_sync_skipped_disabled_in_dem: Last manual sync: skipped (disabled in Demo Mode)
settings_page.last_manual_sync_pull_applied_ack_pendin: Last manual sync: pull applied, ACK pending ({{detail}})
settings_page.used_to_highlight_expected_work_window_o: Used to highlight expected work window on timeline.
settings_page.select_preferred_currency_for_project_va: Select preferred currency for project values.
settings_page.adjust_visual_effects_and_performance_op: Adjust visual effects and performance options.
settings_page.turn_off_to_improve_ui_responsiveness_on: Turn off to improve UI responsiveness on slower devices.
settings_page.rules_for_automatic_merging_of_nearby_se: Rules for automatic merging of nearby sessions.
settings_page.sessions_shorter_than_or_equal_to_this_d: Sessions shorter than or equal to this duration will be hidden from the list.
settings_page.automatically_merge_close_sessions_when_: Automatically merge close sessions when app starts.
settings_page.apply_current_merge_gap_to_already_impor: Apply current merge gap to already imported sessions.
settings_page.startup_synchronization_with_remote_serv: Startup synchronization with remote server using snapshot push/pull.
settings_page.allows_the_dashboard_to_exchange_data_sn: Allows the dashboard to exchange data snapshots with the sync server.
settings_page.runs_status_pull_push_after_local_auto_i: Runs status -> pull/push after local auto-import finishes only when online sync is enabled.
settings_page.periodic_sync_after_app_startup_default_: Periodic sync after app startup. Default is every 30 minutes.
settings_page.save_detailed_sync_operations_to_log_fil: Save detailed sync operations to log file for debugging.
settings_page.paste_the_raw_token_without_bearer_prefi: Paste the raw token (without 'Bearer ' prefix and without quotes)
settings_page.enter_the_raw_token_the_app_will_add_the: Enter the raw token; the app will add the Bearer header automatically.
settings_page.token_set_auto: Token set automatically during license activation.
settings_page.generated_automatically_and_used_to_iden: Generated automatically and used to identify this machine during sync.
settings_page.online_sync_is_disabled_while_demo_mode_: Online sync is disabled while Demo Mode is active.
settings_page.server_payload_was_cleaned_up_after_acks: Server payload was cleaned up after ACKs. Local reseed/export is required.
settings_page.projects_inactive_for_a_set_period_are_a: Projects inactive for a set period are automatically frozen and hidden from session assignment lists.
settings_page.number_of_days_without_activity_after_wh: Number of days without activity after which a project is automatically frozen.
settings_page.switch_dashboard_data_source_to_a_separa: Switch dashboard data source to a separate demo database file (persists after restart).
settings_page.applies_to_the_whole_dashboard_app_reads: Applies to the whole dashboard app (reads/writes/imports) and switches to a separate SQLite file. In demo mode, live daily refresh reads from fake_data and expects fake in the JSON filename (for example 2026-02-22_fake.json).
settings_page.demo_mode_is_active_new_imports_changes_: Demo mode is active. New imports/changes will affect the demo database.
settings_page.hidden_by_default_to_avoid_accidental_cl: Hidden by default to avoid accidental clicks.
settings_page.deletes_all_imported_sessions_and_histor: Deletes all imported sessions and history from local database.
settings_page.unsaved_changes_confirm: You have unsaved settings changes. Leave this screen without saving?

help_page.is_an_advanced_time_tracking_ecosystem_t: is an advanced time tracking ecosystem that works discreetly in the background, letting you focus on what really matters.
help_page.unlike_traditional_tools_timeflow_intell: Unlike traditional tools, TIMEFLOW intelligently analyzes window activity, processes, and files to precisely assign your time to the correct projects.
help_page.the_timeflow_daemon_monitors_used_applic: The TIMEFLOW Daemon monitors used applications and active documents without your intervention.
help_page.a_local_machine_learning_ml_engine_learn: A local machine learning (ML) engine learns your habits without sending any data to the cloud.
help_page.get_instant_insight_into_the_actual_valu: Get instant insight into the actual value of your work thanks to the rate and estimate system.
help_page.your_data_is_your_property_everything_is: Your data is your property. Everything is stored locally in a secure SQLite database.
help_page.fast_timeflow_setup_for_a_new_install_an: Fast TIMEFLOW setup for a new install and first launch.
help_page.step_by_step_guidance_from_exe_preparati: Step by step guidance from .exe preparation to launching the Daemon.
help_page.configuration_of_project_folders_and_app: Configuration of project folders and app processes to be tracked.
help_page.your_monitored_applications_list_should_: Your monitored applications list should not stay empty. If you add no processes, TIMEFLOW pauses tracking instead of falling back to monitor-all.
help_page.first_session_assignment_and_local_ai_on: First-session assignment and local AI onboarding instructions.
help_page.accessible_from_the_sidebar_rocket_icon_: Accessible from the sidebar rocket icon and from the Help screen.
help_page.automatically_clears_the_first_run_hint_: Automatically clears the first-run hint after finishing the tutorial.
help_page.the_full_tutorial_walks_through_installa: The full tutorial walks through installation and configuration end-to-end.
help_page.quick_overview_of_your_current_activity_: Quick overview of your current activity and key performance indicators.
help_page.integrated_metrics_cards_total_tracked_t: Integrated metrics cards (total tracked time, number of apps, active projects).
help_page.interactive_timeline_with_hourly_view_to: Interactive timeline with hourly view (today) or daily view (longer periods).
help_page.top_5_projects_charts_and_analysis_of_mo: 'Top 5 Projects' charts and analysis of most used applications.
help_page.quick_time_range_switching_today_week_mo: Quick time range switching: Today, Week, Month, All Time.
help_page.timeline_visualization_mode_shows_your_e: Timeline visualization mode – shows your engagement in real-time.
help_page.notifications_on_auto_import_status_and_: Notifications on auto-import status and potential data read errors.
help_page.sidebar_status_indicators_act_as_a_contr: Sidebar status indicators act as a control center: they show daemon, sync, AI, and backup state, and clicking them opens the relevant diagnostic screen.
help_page.refresh_button_synchronizing_data_direct: Refresh button synchronizing data directly from the running Daemon.
help_page.unassigned_sessions_banner_shows_the_cou: Unassigned sessions banner – shows the count of today's unassigned sessions with app count and total duration. Clicking navigates to Sessions.
help_page.discovered_projects_notification_after_f: Discovered projects notification – when a scheduled startup folder scan finds new projects, the dashboard shows a banner with the newly detected items. You can dismiss it or open project management.
help_page.add_manual_session_from_timeline_click_o: Add manual session from timeline – click on the dashboard timeline to quickly add a manual session (e.g. meeting, call) without navigating to Sessions.
help_page.all_projects_overview_chart_showing_work: All Projects overview – chart showing work time distribution across all projects for the selected period.
help_page.timeline_states_loading_empty_and_error_: Timeline states – dashboard charts show loading, no-data, and error states instead of an empty panel when the selected range has no results or refresh fails.
help_page.detailed_list_of_all_activity_blocks_reg: Detailed list of all activity blocks registered in the system.
help_page.adding_comments_and_notes_right_click_a_: Adding comments and notes – right-click a session to create a description.
help_page.rate_multipliers_define_rate_x2_or_custo: Rate multipliers – define rate x2 or custom for higher-value work.
help_page.ai_suggestions_review_and_approve_or_rej: AI Suggestions – review and approve (or reject) project suggestions generated by AI.
help_page.manual_session_addition_register_meeting: Manual session addition – register meetings, calls, or offline work.
help_page.multi_day_manual_sessions_the_allow_sess: Multi-day manual sessions – the "Allow session across multiple days" option lets you save longer events that cross midnight.
help_page.batch_assign_select_multiple_sessions_an: Batch Assign – select multiple sessions and assign them to a project with one click.
help_page.session_badges_show_ai_assignment_confid: Session badges – icons next to a row show AI assignment, suggestion confidence, score breakdown availability, and split readiness. Click the score badge to expand details.
help_page.split_session_from_the_context_menu_or_s: Split Session – from the context menu or scissors icon, split a session into multiple parts (max 5) and assign each to a different project. AI automatically analyzes file activity and suggests the split.
help_page.split_ai_uses_overlap_first_then_learned: Split suggestions first measure real file-activity overlap inside the session window, and when that is not enough, AI falls back to learned app, time, and file patterns. A confirmed split strengthens future suggestions in proportion to each part's share.
help_page.view_modes_detailed_full_file_logs_compa: View modes: Detailed (full file logs), Compact (apps and sessions list only), and AI Data (precise statistics and AI model reasoning).
help_page.daily_weekly_range_mode_switch_the_sessi: Daily/Weekly range mode – switch the sessions list between daily and weekly views to review longer periods faster.
help_page.sorting_and_filtering_by_application_pro: Sorting and filtering by application, project, date, and duration.
help_page.batch_assign_from_project_header_the_gro: Batch assign from project header – the group context menu lets you assign or unassign the whole block of sessions for a project.
help_page.unassigned_only_filter_quickly_focus_on_: "Unassigned only" filter – quickly focus on sessions that still need assignment. In this mode the list shows sessions from all dates, regardless of the currently visible Daily/Weekly range.
help_page.session_split_settings_tolerance_coeffic: Session split settings – tolerance coefficient (0.2–1.0), max projects per session (2–5), automatic split. Configure in Settings > Session Split.
help_page.project_list_modes_in_the_assignment_men: Project list modes in the assignment menu: Active A-Z, Newest -> Top -> Rest, and Top -> Newest -> Rest. They speed up selection when you have many projects.
help_page.scissors_icon_appears_on_sessions_where_: Scissors icon – appears on sessions where AI detected file activity across multiple projects. Click to split the session based on AI suggestions.
help_page.auto_split_runs_in_cycles_it_starts_afte: Auto-split runs in cycles: it starts after import and repeats every 60 seconds. In one pass it analyzes up to 50 unassigned sessions and performs at most 5 splits.
help_page.sessions_refresh_when_filters_or_data_ch: The sessions list refreshes when filters change, when real data changes, and when you return to the TIMEFLOW window, without a separate 15s timer on this page.
help_page.the_ai_data_view_presents_the_model_s_tr: The "AI Data" view presents the model's "train of thought" for assigned or suggested sessions. Here is how to read its metrics:
help_page.expressed_in_percentage_0_100_it_s_the_m: Expressed in percentage (0-100%), it's the model's certainty about its choice. Above the set threshold (e.g., 40%), the model generates a suggestion.
help_page.the_number_of_similar_past_sessions_you_: The number of similar past sessions you manually approved. This is the hardest proof for the model – the more evidence, the more certain the decision.
help_page.raw_mathematical_and_probabilistic_match: Raw mathematical and probabilistic match scores calculated by the ML engine. Mainly used for diagnostic purposes.
help_page.keywords_from_filenames_windows_or_websi: Keywords from filenames, windows, or website titles (and general context like time of day) that the model identified as directly linked to that specific project.
help_page.negative_points_if_the_model_detected_tr: Negative points if the model detected traits suggesting the assignment could be murky despite other strong signals.
help_page.managing_task_structure_and_intelligent_: Managing task structure and intelligent automation of project detection.
help_page.freezing_hide_inactive_projects_to_keep_: Freezing – hide inactive projects to keep them from cluttering session assignment.
help_page.auto_freezing_the_system_automatically_f: Auto-freezing – the system automatically 'freezes' projects unused for a specified number of days.
help_page.unfreezing_use_the_flame_icon_to_restore: Unfreezing – use the flame icon to restore a project to the active tasks list.
help_page.folder_sync_timeflow_scans_paths_on_ever: Folder Sync – TIMEFLOW periodically scans configured paths during dashboard startup and automatically adds new projects (including .git repositories). When new items are found, a discovered-projects notification appears on the dashboard.
help_page.candidate_detection_the_system_suggests_: Candidate Detection – the system suggests project creation based on folder activity.
help_page.duplicate_name_marker_projects_with_simi: Duplicate name marker – projects with very similar names are marked with a `D` badge, and the list shows how many potential collisions exist in the current tab.
help_page.root_folders_manage_disk_locations_that_: Root folders – manage disk locations that TIMEFLOW should monitor.
help_page.exclude_remove_projects_from_view_withou: Exclude – remove projects from view without permanently deleting them from the database.
help_page.search_filter_projects_by_name_or_folder: Search – filter projects by name or folder path in real time.
help_page.color_change_click_the_color_dot_on_the_: Color change – click the color dot on the project card to change the project color (preset palette + custom color).
help_page.project_card_projectpage_full_project_vi: Project card (ProjectPage) – full project view: data compaction, time reset, inline name edit, timeline with comments, manual sessions, and report generation.
help_page.project_time_is_consistent_across_projec: Consistent project time – project lists, ProjectPage, estimates, and reports all use the same deduplicated clock time for overlapping sessions.
help_page.project_page_daily_timeline_comments_man: ProjectPage workflow – the project detail view combines the daily timeline, recent comments, manual sessions, compaction, and direct report generation in one place.
help_page.saved_view_persist_your_preferred_sortin: Saved View – persist your preferred sorting and presentation mode for lists/timeline so it comes back when you reopen the screen.
help_page.project_data_compaction_action_in_the_pr: Project data compaction – action in the project view that reduces data size without losing time summaries.
help_page.recent_comments_card_in_the_project_view: Recent Comments – card in the project view showing latest session comments with date and app context.
help_page.project_manual_sessions_dedicated_card_i: Project manual sessions – dedicated card in the project view for adding, editing, and deleting manual sessions assigned to this project.
help_page.chart_context_menu_right_click_on_a_day_: Chart context menu – right-click on a day in the project timeline to see the list of sessions for that date.
help_page.project_timeline_states_loading_empty_an: Project timeline states – the project chart shows loading, no-data, and error states, so you can immediately tell whether the range is empty or the refresh failed.
help_page.business_module_for_precise_conversion_o: Business module for precise conversion of time into finances.
help_page.global_hourly_rate_configuration_and_spe: Global hourly rate configuration and specific rates for chosen projects.
help_page.includes_session_multipliers_in_the_fina: Includes session multipliers in the final project valuation.
help_page.manual_session_valuation_meetings_and_ca: Manual session valuation – meetings and calls are added to the project budget.
help_page.project_profitability_analysis_over_time: Project profitability analysis over time (monthly and yearly views).
help_page.visual_breakdown_into_daily_and_weekly_e: Visual breakdown into daily and weekly earnings.
help_page.ability_to_compare_time_value_spent_on_d: Ability to compare time value spent on different task groups.
help_page.project_rate_reset_button_to_reset_a_pro: Project rate reset – button to reset a project-specific rate back to the global rate.
help_page.multiplier_badge_each_project_shows_the_: Multiplier badge – each project shows the count of sessions with a rate multiplier. Clicking navigates to Sessions filtered by that project.
help_page.estimates_loading_and_empty_states_the_t: Loading and empty states – the estimates table shows a loading state while recalculating for a new range and a clear message when no project time is available.
help_page.per_row_save_indicator_when_you_save_a_p: Per-row save indicator – when you save a project-specific rate, TIMEFLOW disables that row and shows a saving indicator until the update finishes.
help_page.managing_the_list_of_detected_software_a: Managing the list of detected software and processes.
help_page.full_list_of_applications_with_activity_: Full list of applications with activity history and time statistics.
help_page.app_aliases_change_process_names_e_g_cmd: App Aliases – change process names (e.g., 'cmd.exe') to readable ones (e.g., 'Terminal').
help_page.tracking_block_remove_data_for_applicati: Tracking Block – remove data for applications you don't want to track.
help_page.app_data_archiving_reset_tracking_time_w: App Data Archiving – reset tracking time without deleting the app definition.
help_page.directly_assign_an_entire_application_to: Directly assign an entire application to a specific project.
help_page.monitored_applications_section_for_manag: Monitored Applications – section for managing processes actively tracked by the Daemon. Add new processes (exe_name + display name), remove unwanted ones, rename them. The monitored app count is shown as a badge.
help_page.sync_from_apps_button_copies_detected_ap: The `Sync from apps` button copies detected rows from Applications into the monitored list, so you do not have to retype exe names.
help_page.app_colors_inline_picker_with_preset_pal: App colors – inline picker with preset palette and custom color. Click the color next to an app name to change it.
help_page.imported_badge_label_on_applications_tha: "Imported" badge – label on applications that came from imported data rather than live monitoring.
help_page.list_pagination_for_large_app_lists_a_lo: List pagination – for large app lists, a "Load more" control is available to keep the view performant.
help_page.deep_visualization_of_your_habits_and_wo: Deep visualization of your habits and work intensity.
help_page.activity_heatmaps_hourly_and_daily_visua: Activity Heatmaps – hourly and daily visualization of your engagement.
help_page.monthly_view_with_week_numbers_facilitat: Monthly view with week numbers – facilitates planning and retrospection.
help_page.intensity_analysis_charts_showing_what_h: Intensity Analysis – charts showing what hours you work most effectively.
help_page.stacked_bar_charts_percentage_share_of_p: Stacked Bar Charts – percentage share of projects in your total work time.
help_page.timeline_project_view_detailed_timeline_: Timeline Project View – detailed timeline broken down by specific tasks.
help_page.range_toolbar_daily_weekly_monthly_butto: Range toolbar – Daily / Weekly / Monthly buttons switch the data view granularity.
help_page.period_navigation_previous_next_arrows_l: Period navigation – Previous/Next arrows let you move between days, weeks, or months.
help_page.interactive_legend_project_list_with_col: Interactive legend – project list with colors and times; clicking an item filters the chart to the selected project.
help_page.proprietary_local_ml_engine_rust_analyzi: Proprietary local ML engine (Rust) analyzing app context, time of day, and file/window tokens. Works 100% offline.
help_page.auto_safe_mode_secure_batch_session_assi: Auto-safe Mode – secure, batch session assignment (requires confidence and evidence thresholds).
help_page.rollback_ability_to_undo_the_last_batch_: Rollback – ability to undo the last batch assignment run by the AI.
help_page.confidence_policy_set_how_certain_the_mo: Confidence Policy – set how certain the model must be to automatically assign data.
help_page.learning_center_every_manual_correction_: Learning Center – every manual correction of yours becomes a new lesson for the model.
help_page.training_notifications_the_system_notifi: Training Notifications – the system notifies you when new knowledge has been gathered.
help_page.train_now_button_is_highlighted_when_sid: "Train Now" highlight – when the AI status in the sidebar shows "New data", the Model Status card highlights the training action.
help_page.snooze_training_reminder_postpone_the_tr: Snooze Training Reminder – postpone the training reminder for 24h when you want to finish work without interruption.
help_page.modes_off_manual_only_suggest_ai_hints_a: Modes: Off (manual only), Suggest (AI hints), Auto-safe (automation).
help_page.training_blacklists_exclude_selected_app: Training blacklists – exclude selected applications and folders from model training to avoid contaminating the learning data.
help_page.ai_progress_quality_metrics_panel_shows_: AI progress and quality – the metrics panel shows feedback trends, precision, auto-assigned results, and coverage.
help_page.training_horizon_set_how_many_days_of_hi: Training Horizon – set the day range (for example 30-730) from which the model takes training data.
help_page.decay_half_life_controls_how_quickly_old: Decay half-life: controls how quickly old training data loses influence on the model. At 90 days (default), data from 90 days ago contributes half as much as today's data. Lower values (14-60) make the model adapt faster to workflow changes; higher values (180-365) give more stable predictions but react slower.
help_page.auto_safe_limit_control_the_maximum_numb: Auto-safe limit – control the maximum number of sessions processed in a single Auto-safe run.
help_page.session_indicators_configure_indicators_: Session Indicators – configure the indicators shown next to session rows (AI, suggestions, score breakdown).
help_page.k_100_privacy_the_ml_engine_runs_locally: 100% privacy – the ML engine runs locally in Rust, does not use external APIs (such as ChatGPT), and does not require internet access.
help_page.model_status_card_diagnostic_panel_with_: "Model Status" card – a diagnostic panel with 6 tiles: current mode, training state (idle/in progress), last training date, correction count since training, last training metrics (samples/time), and the last Auto-safe run.
help_page.ai_progress_charts_two_charts_1_feedback: AI progress charts – two charts: (1) Feedback trend – stacked bar chart with Accept, Reject, and Manual corrections per day; (2) Auto-safe runs vs rollback – assigned bars + runs and rollbacks lines. Default window: 30 days.
help_page.summary_metrics_4_tiles_ai_precision_cor: Summary metrics – 4 tiles: AI precision (% correct suggestions), Total feedback, Auto-safe assignments, Detected path coverage. Additional indicators: title_history and activity_type coverage.
help_page.reset_ai_knowledge_button_that_clears_al: Reset AI knowledge – button that clears all learned model knowledge (with confirmation). Useful after a major project structure change.
help_page.how_to_train_and_configure_card_built_in: "How to train and configure" card – built-in guide with three sections: when to train, what parameters mean, and recommended starting settings.
help_page.ai_status_uses_central_background_diagno: The AI model status uses shared TIMEFLOW diagnostics, and metrics refresh when you open the screen, run AI actions, data changes, or return to the app window.
help_page.timeflow_uses_a_proprietary_local_machin: TIMEFLOW uses a proprietary, local machine learning model (Local ML) for classification, written in Rust. It's not an external AI (like ChatGPT), but an algorithm running 100% on your computer, ensuring full privacy.
help_page.the_model_analyzes_your_historical_manua: The model analyzes your historical, manual session assignments to projects. During 'training', it builds statistical tables based on three main pillars:
help_page.which_programs_you_assign_to_which_proje: Which programs you assign to which projects.
help_page.time_of_day_and_day_of_the_week_your_wor: Time of day and day of the week (your work habits).
help_page.keywords_extracted_from_file_names_and_w: Keywords extracted from file names and windows (the strongest signal).
help_page.the_model_doesn_t_guess_blindly_for_each: The model doesn't guess 'blindly' – for each unassigned session it calculates:
help_page.a_value_from_0_to_1_determined_by_a_sigm: A value from 0 to 1 determined by a sigmoid function.
help_page.the_number_of_historical_proofs_confirmi: The number of historical 'proofs' confirming the decision.
help_page.the_difference_between_the_best_and_seco: The difference between the best and second match (protects against errors).
help_page.suggests_a_project_in_the_menu_requires_: Suggests a project in the menu (requires >60% confidence).
help_page.automatically_assigns_sessions_requires_: Automatically assigns sessions (requires >85% confidence and strong evidence).
help_page.keep_this_mode_the_ai_will_suggest_conne: Keep this mode. The AI will suggest connections/categories but won't assign them automatically. Your manual approvals (or rejections/corrections) are the most crucial element in building the model's "knowledge". (In auto mode, the model wouldn't ask your opinion in some cases, losing a potential chance for confirmation).
help_page.lowering_this_threshold_means_the_model_: Lowering this threshold means the model will make suggestions even when it's not super confident. Consequence: you'll get more suggestions, and by correcting the wrong ones, the model will learn to distinguish harder cases much faster. But if you feel "spammed" by nonsensical suggestions, slowly increase it to 0.6.
help_page.feedback_weight_determines_how_strongly_: Feedback weight determines how strongly a single correction from you affects the model's subsequent decisions. Higher value = the model adapts faster to your fresh behaviors and corrections. Important: if the weight is too high (e.g., 50), the model might "go crazy" after a single accidental mistake. A value of 10-15 allows for efficient learning while being a relatively stable option.
help_page.k_4_criteria_for_auto_safe_for_the_futur: 4. Criteria for Auto-safe (for the future / safety)
help_page.if_after_the_learning_period_you_want_to: If after the learning period you want to enable auto-safe mode, where AI solves obvious cases by itself:
help_page.keep_it_high_let_it_automate_only_absolu: (keep it high, let it automate only absolute certainties).
help_page.increase_from_3_this_means_the_model_mus: (increase from 3. This means the model must have strong confirmation in at least 5 similar, previously user-approved sessions to act without your consent).
help_page.all_model_data_is_stored_in_your_local_s: All model data is stored in your local SQLite database (assignment_model_state, etc.), so the system gets smarter with each of your corrections.
help_page.importing_exporting_and_organizing_the_k: Importing, exporting, and organizing the knowledge base.
help_page.zip_export_quick_archiving_of_the_entire: ZIP Export – quick archiving of the entire database or selected projects to .zip.
help_page.json_import_loading_daily_reports_genera: JSON Import – loading daily reports generated by the Daemon.
help_page.import_page_separate_screen_for_drag_dro: Import page – separate screen for drag & drop JSON imports, imported file history, and archive cleanup after processing.
help_page.archive_import_zip_package_validation_be: Archive import (.zip) – package validation before import and safe merge of historical data.
help_page.system_maintenance_cleaning_old_records_: System Maintenance – cleaning old records and optimizing file size.
help_page.operation_history_insight_into_when_and_: Operation History – insight into when and what data was modified.
help_page.backup_restore_database_manual_backups_r: Backup/Restore database – manual backups, restore from file, and quick access to the SQLite database folder.
help_page.data_history_refreshes_after_real_data_c: Data History refreshes after real data changes and when you return to the app window, so it no longer keeps a constant background polling loop.
help_page.import_page_detail_what_it_does: What it does: the Import page lets you drop JSON files, inspect the history of processed imports, and clean the archive after processing.
help_page.import_page_detail_when_to_use: When to use it: open it after a manual daemon export or whenever you want to verify whether a package has already been imported.
help_page.import_page_detail_limitations: Limitations and consequences: this screen only manages import and archive data, and once an archived file is deleted TIMEFLOW cannot restore it without the original source file.
help_page.create_configurable_project_reports_for_: Create configurable project reports for print and PDF export.
help_page.template_system_create_duplicate_and_man: Template system – create, duplicate, and manage multiple report templates. Choose a template before generating.
help_page.report_template_editor_choose_report_sec: Report template editor – choose report sections and their order (header, stats, financials, apps, sessions, boosts, manual sessions, comments, AI).
help_page.timeflow_logo_and_version_the_report_hea: TIMEFLOW logo and version – the report header includes the app logo and version number, visible in PDF prints.
help_page.report_generation_button_in_the_top_tool: Report generation – button in the top toolbar of the project page. Select a template, then preview, print, or export to PDF.
help_page.reportview_full_screen_report_preview_wi: ReportView – full-screen report preview (without the side panel) optimized for print and PDF export.
help_page.report_view_toolbar_focuses_on_preview_p: ReportView toolbar – clean full-screen preview with Print / PDF action; use it to verify layout, scaling, and page breaks before export.
help_page.report_work_time_uses_the_same_deduplica: Consistent work time in reports – reports show the same work time as project and estimate views, while multipliers affect financial value instead of hour totals.
help_page.additional_sections_boosts_sessions_with: Additional sections: Boosts (sessions with time multiplier), Manual sessions (manually added), Comments, AI Statistics.
help_page.section_reordering_up_down_arrows_on_eac: Section reordering – up/down arrows on each section in the template editor let you set a custom order of report elements.
help_page.preview_loading_state_when_switching_tem: Preview loading state – when switching templates or rebuilding the section layout, the preview panel shows a temporary loading state instead of stale content.
help_page.empty_templates_state_if_no_report_templ: Empty templates state – if no report templates are available, TIMEFLOW shows a clear empty state with a button to create a new template.
help_page.reportview_detail_what_it_does: What it does: ReportView opens the generated report in full-screen mode (without the sidebar) so you can review the final layout exactly as it will appear on paper or in a PDF file.
help_page.reportview_detail_when_to_use: When to use it: open ReportView when you want to print the report, export it to PDF, or present it on screen. Use it to verify page breaks, scaling, and section order before sharing.
help_page.reportview_detail_how_to_print: How to print / export PDF: click the Print / PDF button in the ReportView toolbar — this opens the system print dialog where you can choose a printer or select "Save as PDF" as the destination.
help_page.reportview_detail_limitations: Limitations: ReportView is read-only — to change the report content or section order, go back to the report template editor on the project page.
help_page.control_center_for_the_background_proces: Control center for the background process responsible for data collection.
help_page.status_control_diagnostics_monitor_if_th: Status Control & Diagnostics – monitor if the time tracking system is working correctly.
help_page.service_management_start_stop_and_restar: Service Management – start, stop, and restart the Daemon directly from the dashboard.
help_page.windows_autostart_automatic_startup_of_t: Windows Autostart – automatic startup of TIMEFLOW upon system login.
help_page.real_time_logs_preview_of_the_event_log_: Real-time Logs – preview of the event log to identify issues.
help_page.version_insight_information_on_the_compa: Version Insight – information on the compatibility of Daemon and Dashboard versions.
help_page.localization_the_daemon_tray_menu_automa: Localization – the Daemon tray menu automatically switches between PL/EN based on the language setting in the Dashboard (Settings > Language).
help_page.monitor_all_fallback_if_the_monitored_pr: An empty monitored process list pauses tracking. TIMEFLOW does not switch to monitor-all in that case, so configure the Applications tab before production use.
help_page.monitored_apps_with_empty_window_titles_: Monitored apps with an empty window title are still counted as work time. In that case TIMEFLOW may not know the document or file name, but the app session itself is still tracked.
help_page.unassigned_sessions_indicator_badge_with: Unassigned sessions indicator – badge with the count of sessions awaiting assignment, visible directly on the Daemon screen.
help_page.log_auto_refresh_button_to_enable_automa: Log auto-refresh – button to enable automatic real-time log preview updates.
help_page.log_auto_refresh_runs_only_while_the_dae: Log auto-refresh runs only while the Daemon screen is open and the TIMEFLOW window is visible; when you return, the app immediately fetches the latest log tail.
help_page.log_coloring_error_and_warn_lines_are_hi: Log coloring – ERROR and WARN lines are highlighted with color for quick problem identification.
help_page.full_control_over_application_configurat: Full control over application configuration and security.
help_page.working_hours_define_work_hours_affects_: Working Hours – define work hours (affects timeline color scheme).
help_page.session_management_set_session_merging_t: Session Management – set session merging threshold (Gap Fill) and ignore short blocks.
help_page.auto_rebuild_on_startup_automatically_re: Auto-rebuild on startup – automatically rebuild/merge sessions on application startup.
help_page.freeze_threshold_configure_the_number_of: Freeze Threshold – configure the number of days after which projects are frozen.
help_page.currency_choose_valuation_currency_pln_u: Currency – choose valuation currency (PLN/USD/EUR) used across financial modules.
help_page.ui_language_pl_en_language_switch_for_th: UI language (PL/EN) – language switch for the whole Dashboard and Daemon tray menu.
help_page.online_sync_set_up_synchronization_with_: Online Sync – set up synchronization with an external server (URL, User ID, Token).
help_page.device_id_a_device_identifier_is_generat: Device ID – a device identifier is generated when sync settings are saved and distinguishes this specific machine on the server.
help_page.the_sync_token_is_stored_in_rust_side_se: The sync token is stored in Rust-side secure storage; the dashboard also migrates an older token from localStorage when needed.
help_page.sync_on_startup_runs_only_when_online_sy: Sync on startup – runs synchronization after Dashboard startup only when online sync is enabled.
help_page.auto_sync_interval_configure_automatic_s: Auto-sync interval – configure automatic synchronization interval (1–1440 min).
help_page.ack_statuses_in_online_sync_the_status_a: ACK statuses in Online Sync – the status area shows whether local changes are still waiting for server confirmation (ACK pending).
help_page.online_sync_status_panel_shows_revision_: Online Sync status panel – shows server/local revision, hash, pending ACK retries, and reseed warnings when the remote snapshot was pruned.
help_page.server_snapshot_pruned_scenario_if_the_s: server_snapshot_pruned scenario – if the server payload was cleaned after ACK, run a local reseed/export to rebuild sync state.
help_page.sync_logging_you_can_enable_file_logging: Sync logging – you can enable file logging for synchronization to diagnose pull/push issues and share logs with support.
help_page.demo_mode_switch_to_a_demo_database_test: Demo Mode – switch to a demo database (test without affecting real data).
help_page.demo_mode_and_sync_when_switched_to_the_: Demo Mode and Sync – when switched to the demo database, online synchronization is disabled to prevent mixing test and production data.
help_page.demo_mode_uses_a_separate_sqlite_file_an: Demo mode uses a separate SQLite file and reads live daily refreshes from the fake_data directory; JSON filenames should then include fake, for example 2026-02-22_fake.json.
help_page.auto_optimize_db_schedule_automatic_sqli: Auto Optimize DB – schedule automatic SQLite optimization and run optimization manually.
help_page.emergency_clear_option_to_completely_cle: Emergency Clear – option to completely clear the database and settings.
help_page.appearance_performance_disable_chart_ani: Appearance & Performance – disable chart animations to improve UI responsiveness.
help_page.highlight_color_choose_the_highlight_col: Highlight Color – choose the highlight color for working-hours zones on charts.
help_page.bughunter_the_bug_icon_in_the_sidebar_al: BugHunter – the bug icon in the sidebar allows quick bug reporting with attachments.
help_page.session_split_settings_in_a_dedicated_ca: Session Split – settings in a dedicated card: tolerance coefficient (0.2–1.0), max projects per session (2–5), and auto-split toggle. Higher tolerance means more conservative splits.
help_page.danger_zone_two_step_confirmation_first_: Danger Zone – two-step confirmation: first "arm" the button, then click to confirm. Protects against accidental database wipe.
help_page.manual_session_rebuild_button_in_the_ses: Manual session rebuild – button in the Session Management card forces session re-merge (gap fill) without restarting the app.
help_page.unsaved_changes_warning_before_leaving_s: Unsaved changes in Settings – when you try to leave without saving, TIMEFLOW asks for confirmation. Closing or refreshing the window also triggers the browser warning.
help_page.dev_log_management_centralized_log_viewe: Dev / Log Management – centralized log viewer and configuration for all daemon channels (Daemon, LAN Sync, Online Sync, Dashboard). Set log levels, max file size with auto-rotation, view live log content, and clear individual log files.
help_page.project_page_detail_what_it_does: What it does: ProjectPage combines the project timeline, recent comments, manual sessions, data compaction, time reset, and report generation in one view.
help_page.project_page_detail_when_to_use: When to use: open this view when you want to review one project end-to-end instead of jumping between multiple screens.
help_page.project_page_detail_limitations: Limitations and consequences: reset time and compaction affect only the current project, and the visible timeline depends on the selected range and available data.
help_page.manual_session_dialog_detail_what_it_doe: What it does: the manual session form lets you add meetings, calls, and other offline work to a selected project and optionally link it to a specific application.
help_page.manual_session_dialog_detail_when_to_use: When to use: use it when TIMEFLOW could not track the time automatically or when you need to add activity after the fact.
help_page.manual_session_dialog_detail_limitations: Limitations and consequences: the end time must be later than the start time, and crossing midnight requires enabling the multi-day session option.
help_page.online_sync_setup_what_it_does: What it does: Online Sync exchanges TIMEFLOW data snapshots with a server so you can restore or move state between devices.
help_page.online_sync_setup_how_to_start: How to start: enter the server URL, User ID, and raw Token, save settings, wait for Device ID generation, and only then run `Sync now` or enable `Sync on startup`.
help_page.online_sync_setup_when_to_use: When to use: enable it when you want off-device backups or want to sync work between multiple machines.
help_page.online_sync_setup_limitations: Limitations and consequences: Demo Mode disables sync, an invalid token blocks push/pull, and a reseed warning means you need to rebuild state with a local export or a fresh seed.
help_page.online_sync_daemon_mode: Daemon-based sync (LAN Sync Protocol): When the TIMEFLOW daemon is running, online sync can use the 13-step session protocol with SFTP transfer. This provides encrypted file transfer, progress tracking, and automatic merge — same protocol as LAN sync but through a remote server. Configure the encryption key in sync settings.
help_page.delta_sync_description: Since the Delta Sync version: the system transmits only modified synchronization packets for old and new sessions, saving up to 95% of bandwidth usage.
help_page.license_activation: License activation: paste your license key in Online Sync → License section and click Activate. Your device will be registered in the group on the server. After activation the dashboard shows the plan, group, device count and expiration date.
help_page.lan_sync_description: Peer-to-peer synchronization between TIMEFLOW instances on the same local network, without an intermediary server.
help_page.lan_sync_udp_discovery: UDP Discovery – TIMEFLOW automatically broadcasts its presence on the local network (port 47892) every 30 seconds. Other instances are detected without manual configuration.
help_page.lan_sync_http_server: Embedded HTTP Server – when LAN Sync is enabled, a local HTTP server starts (default port 47891) and serves /lan/ping, /lan/status, /lan/pull, /lan/push endpoints for data exchange.
help_page.lan_sync_delta_merge: Delta Merge – synchronization transfers only changes (projects, applications, sessions, manual sessions, tombstones) since the last sync. Conflicts are resolved by the most recent updated_at timestamp.
help_page.lan_sync_auto_sync: Auto-sync on peer discovery – when enabled, TIMEFLOW automatically synchronizes as soon as another instance is found on the network.
help_page.lan_sync_peer_notification: Peer notification – a floating notification appears when a new TIMEFLOW instance is detected. You can sync immediately or dismiss the notification.
help_page.lan_sync_sidebar_indicator: Sidebar indicator – the LAN status in the sidebar shows the name of a discovered peer (or 'No peers'). Click it to open LAN Sync settings.
help_page.lan_sync_port_config: Server port – configurable port for the embedded HTTP server (1024–65535). Both devices must be on the same LAN/Wi-Fi.
help_page.lan_sync_firewall: Automatic Firewall – on first startup, the daemon creates Windows Firewall rules for UDP 47892 (discovery) and TCP 47891 (sync server) for ALL network profiles (private, public, domain). If the daemon lacks admin privileges, a warning is logged and you may need to add the rules manually.
help_page.lan_sync_subnet_broadcast: Subnet Broadcast – discovery reads actual subnet masks from network interfaces and calculates correct broadcast addresses. Discover responses are sent directly to the requester (unicast) for reliability when broadcast is blocked.
help_page.lan_sync_background_interval: Background Sync Interval – when a sync interval is configured (4h/8h/12h/24h/48h), TIMEFLOW automatically syncs with the first available online peer in the background, even without manual action.
help_page.online_sync_sse_realtime: Real-time Notifications – when online sync is enabled, TIMEFLOW maintains a live connection to the server. When another device pushes data, your device is instantly notified and pulls the update automatically.
help_page.lan_sync_setup_what_it_does: What it does: LAN Sync exchanges data directly between two TIMEFLOW instances on the same network — no server, no cloud, no account needed.
help_page.lan_sync_setup_how_to_start: How to start: enable LAN Sync in Settings on both machines, make sure they are on the same LAN/Wi-Fi, and wait for peer discovery (up to 30 seconds).
help_page.lan_sync_setup_when_to_use: When to use: use it to quickly synchronize work data between your desktop and laptop, or between team members on the same office network.
help_page.lan_sync_setup_limitations: Limitations: both devices must be on the same local network. Large datasets may take a moment on the first sync.
help_page.lan_sync_master_slave: MASTER/SLAVE Roles – by default, roles are assigned via UDP election at startup (longest uptime wins MASTER). You can override this in Settings > LAN Sync > Device role — set Master or Slave manually. The daemon applies the forced role within 60 seconds.
help_page.lan_sync_sync_markers: Sync Markers – after each successful sync, a SHA256 marker is stored in SQLite. Matching markers enable efficient delta sync; mismatched markers trigger a full sync.
help_page.lan_sync_scheduled: Scheduled Sync – configure the sync interval (4h/8h/12h/24h/48h or manual) in Settings. Discovery activates only during sync windows to save resources.
help_page.lan_sync_freeze: Database Freeze – during sync, both databases are frozen (no new writes). The daemon buffers activity in memory and flushes after unfreeze. Auto-unfreeze after 5 minutes (safety net).
help_page.lan_sync_backup: Automatic Backup – a database backup is created before every merge. Up to 5 backups are rotated automatically.
help_page.lan_sync_progress: Sync Progress – during synchronization a progress bar shows the current step, transfer speed, and estimated time remaining.
help_page.lan_sync_force_sync: Force Sync – forces a full database merge between both machines, regardless of whether data appears identical. Use when a regular sync doesn't transfer all data or after manual database changes.
help_page.lan_sync_manual_search: Manual Peer Search – if UDP discovery cannot find the other device, enter its IP address manually in Settings > LAN Sync. Your own IP is displayed for reference. The dashboard pings the peer's HTTP server directly, bypassing UDP broadcast entirely.
help_page.bughunter_detail_what_it_does: What it does: BugHunter opens a fast bug report form with subject, description, version number, and attachments.
help_page.bughunter_detail_when_to_use: When to use: send a report right after the issue appears, ideally with short reproduction steps and a screenshot or supporting file.
help_page.bughunter_detail_limitations: Limitations and consequences: each attachment is limited to 5 MB, subject and description are required, and sending the form does not automatically resolve the issue on the support side.
